subject: free-claude-code: run Claude Code free via NVIDIA NIM (40 req/min)
snippet: Claude Code runs free via NVIDIA proxy, GPT Image 2 edits 3D meshes, OpenAI ships multi-agent Python SDK, Sakana AI unlocks diverse LLM outputs Stay updated with today's top AI news, papers, and repos. Signup | Work With Us | Follow on X | …
body_html:
Claude Code runs free via NVIDIA proxy, GPT Image 2 edits 3D meshes, OpenAI ships multi-agent Python SDK, Sakana AI unlocks diverse LLM outputs
Stay updated with today's top AI news, papers, and repos.
Signup | Work With Us | Follow on X | Archive

Hey Aikkara,
We're getting better at using AI. But are we actually getting better?

A new paper coins the LLM Fallacy: the more fluently AI helps you ship code or write prose, the more your brain quietly claims the credit. Confidence inflates, real skill quietly atrophies. It's the GPS effect, applied to your entire career.

Meanwhile, the rest of today's signals are all about making AI cheaper and faster to run. Which means more usage, more reliance, more fallacy.
Together with ·
Today's Author
Lior Alexander. Founder of AlphaSignal and former ML Engineer. Previously built ML systems at Iguazio, Guesty, Enphase, Mila.
Summary

Read time: 6 min 26 sec

▸ Open-source proxy runs Claude Code free using NVIDIA's API at 40 requests per minute

Viktor

▸ An AI coworker with access to your whole stack.

Top Repo

▸ GPT Image 2 now lets you edit 3D meshes directly with text prompts

Datadog

▸ Build Reliable LLM Apps Faster

▸ New paper warns LLM users mistake AI output for their own real skill

Signals

2. OpenAI releases open-source Python SDK for building multi-agent workflows
3. New fine-tuning method teaches LLMs to reason through problems they can't solve directly
4. Sakana AI's prompting trick makes LLMs generate truly random, diverse outputs
5. Open-source LoRA brings authentic CRT terminal animations to video generation
Top Repo
Open-source proxy runs Claude Code free using NVIDIA's API at 40 requests per minute
2,780 Likes

Claude Code is a powerful AI coding tool, but the subscription fees add up fast. A new open-source project called free-claude-code fixes that completely.

Here's the idea: it sits between Claude Code and the internet as a proxy (a middleman that translates requests). It routes Claude Code's API calls to NVIDIA NIM, giving you 40 free requests per minute, or to OpenRouter, LM Studio, or llama.cpp for local use.

What you can do with it:

  • No changes needed to the Claude Code CLI or VSCode extension.
  • Map different models to each tier: heavy tasks, daily coding, and quick edits.
  • Send tasks from your phone via Telegram and watch it work autonomously.
  • The free tier has no expiry, just rate limits.

NVIDIA offers free API access to open-source models through their hosted NIM platform, no credit card needed. Grab a free key, point Claude Code at localhost, and you're coding for free.
READ MORE
forward →
Presented by Viktor
An AI coworker with access to your whole stack.

Yesterday, engineering asked Viktor to review open PRs on GitHub, cross-reference Linear tickets, and flag anything blocking the sprint. Posted to #dev before standup.

Finance asked Viktor to pull Stripe revenue and compare it to Google Ads spend. Formatted PDF in #finance by morning.

Same Slack workspace. One colleague across 3,000+ integrations with read/write access. Persistent memory. No workflows to build.

9,000+ teams. SOC 2 certified. Your data never trains models.
ADD A NEW TEAMMATE
partner with us →
Top Repo
GPT Image 2 now lets you edit 3D meshes directly with text prompts
2,715 Likes

You know how editing a 2D image with AI feels natural now? Describe a change, it happens. CADAM just brought that same experience to 3D models, using GPT Image 2 under the hood.

CADAM is an open-source, browser-based app that lets you type plain English to generate and edit 3D CAD models (the kind used for 3D printing). No CAD software needed. No installs. It runs entirely in your browser.

The new "creative mode" is the interesting part. It pipes your prompt through GPT Image 2 to edit the mesh (the 3D shape made of connected triangles) conversationally, the same way you'd edit a photo. This makes 3D design feel less like engineering and more like a chat.

What you can actually do:

  • Type a description, get a 3D model instantly
  • Upload an image as a reference to guide the shape
  • Tweak dimensions with auto-generated sliders
  • Export as .STL or .SCAD files, ready for printing

The repo has 2.1k stars already. Stack is React, Three.js, OpenSCAD WASM, and Supabase.
READ MORE
forward →

partner with us →
Top Paper
New paper warns LLM users mistake AI output for their own real skill
1,044 Likes

You use AI to write code, fix bugs, draft emails. The output looks great. You feel sharp. But here's the uncomfortable question: was that you, or the AI?

A new paper introduces the "LLM Fallacy" -- the idea that when AI helps you produce something good, your brain quietly takes the credit. You start believing you're more capable than you actually are, because the output felt like yours.

The problem is that AI tools are smooth, fast, and invisible. That low-friction experience hides how much the AI contributed, so your brain fills the gap with confidence you haven't actually earned.

This shows up across four areas:

  • Coding -- shipping AI-written code and feeling like you understand it
  • Writing -- polishing AI drafts and calling it your voice
  • Analysis -- accepting AI conclusions without stress-testing them
  • Languages -- feeling fluent when the AI is doing the heavy lifting

The real risk? Your actual skill gap widens while your confidence grows. Worth keeping in mind next time the AI nails it for you.
READ MORE
forward →
